---
title: "projet_ML"
author: "Adélie, Yelli, Paulina"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---
## Lecture de notre jeu de données
```{r}
data_project=read.csv("Employee.csv")
```

#Analyse Univariée

## Analyse Statistique
```{r}
colnames(data_project)

dim(data_project)
```

#Target
```{r}
target = factor(data_project$LeaveOrNot, labels = c ("Rester", "Partir"))
data_project$LeaveOrNot = as.factor(data_project$LeaveOrNot)
```

#Analyse de la target
```{r}
summary(data_project$LeaveOrNot)

```

#Répartition
```{r}
quantitatives_vars= data.frame(data_project$JoiningYear, 
                             data_project$PaymentTier,
                             data_project$Age,
                             data_project$ExperienceInCurrentDomain)

qualitatives_vars= data.frame(data_project$Education, data_project$City,
                              data_project$Gender, data_project$EverBenched)
```

#### Les Variables quantitatives
```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center"}
for(i in 1:ncol(quantitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Histogramme de ", colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
  hist(quantitatives_vars[,i], col = i+1, main = main_i, 
       xlab = colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
}

for(i in 1:ncol(quantitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Histogramme de ", colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
  boxplot(quantitatives_vars[,i], col = i+1, main = main_i, 
       xlab = colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
}

```
```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center"}
for(i in 1:ncol(qualitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Barplot de ", colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
  barplot(table(qualitatives_vars[,i]), col = 2:10, main = main_i, 
          xlab = colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
}
```

#### Les Variables qualitatives
```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center"}
for(i in 1:ncol(qualitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Barplot de ", colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
  barplot(table(qualitatives_vars[,i]), col = 2:10, main = main_i, 
          xlab = colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
}
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
